events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess duct installation commercial project. Observed field condition: sagging flex (>1/2 in/ft between supports). Work rough-complete. Treat as roughly 70% complete until defects corrected. Need decision fail because non-compliant. Risk? Sagging flex duct can restrict airflow, reduce efficiency, noise, code violation. Commercial project. Risk maybe medium? Could be high if significant airflow restriction and energy code compliance. Let's evaluate. Defect: flex duct sagging exceeds 1/2 inch per foot between supports, violates IMC 603.10? IMC 603.10 requires supports per manufacturer/installation instructions, max sag 1/2 inch per foot between supports. Also SMACNA and Manual D. Component condition installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: 70 until defects corrected, maybe 70. Value not asked, 0. Sound etc 0. Workable empty. Findings: flex duct sag exceeds allowable, inadequate support spacing. Actions: add/reposition supports to limit sag to <=1/2 in/ft, inspect entire flex duct runs, verify support spacing per IMC/SMACNA/Manual D, retest/rebalance airflow if needed, schedule re-inspection. Rationale. References: IMC 603.10 (flex duct installation/support/sag), SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards, ACCA Manual D. Need JSON valid. Ensure no prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Flex duct sag exceeds 1/2 inch per foot between supports",
    "Support spacing or installation does not maintain required duct slope/alignment",
    "Condition violates code and can restrict airflow, increase static pressure, and reduce system performance"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Add or reposition flex duct supports so sag is no more than 1/2 inch per foot between supports",
    "Inspect all flex duct runs for similar sagging and support deficiencies",
    "Verify support spacing and installation comply with IMC 603, SMACNA, and Manual D",
    "Rebalance/test affected zones after corrections if airflow is impacted",
    "Schedule re-inspection before close-in"
  ],
  "rationale": "The flex duct is installed but sags more than the code-allowed 1/2 inch per foot between supports, making the installation non-compliant and defective. Until supports are corrected and the duct is re-inspected, the work cannot pass rough inspection and must be treated as incomplete.",
  "references": [
    "IMC 603 - Flexible duct installation and support requirements",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards",
    "ACCA Manual D - Residential Duct Systems (support and sag guidance)"
  ]
}
